channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court dismisses ED plea to add charges against Farooq Abdullah in JKCA scam
item.description: In a significant development in the Jammu & Kashmir Cricket Association (JKCA) scam, a court in Kashmir has rejected an application by the Enforcement Directorate (ED) to add further charges against N...

item.title: Raja Faisal Rathore elected new AJK Prime Minister after no-confidence vote
item.description: The Azad Jammu and Kashmir (AJK) Legislative Assembly has voted out Prime Minister Chaudhry Anwarul Haq following a no-confidence motion. The motion received backing from 36 lawmakers, including membe...

item.title: Man arrested in connection with the deaths of three women in rural Utah
item.description: Authorities have apprehended a 23-year-old man, identified as Ivan Miller, in relation to the deaths of three women in Wayne County, Utah. The bodies were discovered across multiple locations, with tw...

item.title: EACC Proposes Legal Changes to Prevent Disqualified Leaders From Contesting 2027 Polls
item.description: The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) has asked Parliament to amend parts of the Constitution to make it harder for leaders found guilty of integrity violations to run for office in the 202...

item.title: US and Israeli strikes intensify on Iran and Lebanon amid widening Middle East conflict
item.description: The ongoing war in the Middle East, involving joint US and Israeli military operations against Iran, has escalated significantly. Fresh waves of strikes by American and Israeli forces continued to tar...

item.title: Former O’Sullivan associate emotional during parliamentary questioning on Ipid involvement
item.description: Sarah-Jane Trent, a former associate of forensic investigator Paul O’Sullivan, became emotional during her testimony before parliament’s ad hoc committee on Thursday. The committee is probing allegati...

item.title: Rivers State Girls Trained on Ending FGM and Promoting Health and Rights
item.description: For three days, adolescent girls from Ahoada West Local Government Area of Rivers State gather for a life-changing training to fight Female Genital Mutilation (FGM) and improve their health and wellbe...

item.title: Ottawa Councillor Convicted for Driving Under the Influence
item.description: An Ottawa city councillor has been convicted of impaired driving following a legal proceeding that highlighted the dangers and legal consequences of operating a vehicle under the influence of alcohol ...

item.title: Construction of Hobyo Seaport Continues Despite Security and Regional Trade Challenges
item.description: Somalia inafanya kazi kubwa ya kujenga bandari mpya ya Hobyo, huku wakikabiliana na changamoto za usalama na ushindani wa kibiashara kutoka nchi jirani. Mradi unaosimamiwa na Hobyo Investment Company ...

item.title: Australia’s Position Amid Rising Tensions with Iran Discussed on Full Story Podcast
item.description: The latest edition of the Full Story podcast, hosted by Josephine Tovey, explores Australia's response to the ongoing war with Iran and the wider geopolitical consequences. Guests Gabrielle Jackson an...

item.title: Stocks Contributing Most to Dow Jones Decline Today
item.description: In today's stock market update from The Wall Street Journal's live coverage on March 5, 2026, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced downward pressure from several key components. The article hi...

item.title: UK Covid Inquiry Chair Justifies Duration and Expenditure of £200 Million
item.description: Baroness Heather Hallett, chair of the UK's public inquiry into the Covid-19 pandemic, has defended both the cost and duration of the investigation on the final day of hearings. The inquiry, which for...

item.title: The Enduring Influence of Carl Jung’s Psychological Theories
item.description: Carl Gustav Jung, a Swiss psychiatrist born in 1875, developed a distinct approach to psychology called analytical psychology, diverging from Freudian psychoanalysis. While Freud emphasised a personal...

item.title: BJP rejects claims of India’s involvement in US attack on Iranian warship IRIS Dena
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has strongly dismissed the Congress party’s claims that India bears any responsibility for the US attack on the Iranian naval vessel IRIS Dena. BJP leaders emphasised ...

item.title: Federal Information Minister to Address Fake Video Targeting Journalist Benazir Shah
item.description: Federal Information Minister Atta Tarar has publicly taken notice of a fake AI-generated video aimed at journalist Benazir Shah, calling the incident totally unacceptable and highly condemnable. The i...

item.title: Steve Carell stars in campus comedy 'Rooster' blending humour and family dynamics
item.description: Steve Carell returns to the small screen in 'Rooster', a 10-part comedy series co-created by Bill Lawrence of 'Ted Lasso' and Matt Tarses, co-creator of 'Scrubs'. Set on a university campus, the serie...

item.title: University of Ibadan Financial Report Shows High Spending on Honorarium and Refreshments Over Academic Needs in 2024
item.description: The audited financial statement of the University of Ibadan for the 2024 fiscal year shows serious spending on administrative perks. According to the report, the University spent N653 million on honor...

item.title: Kristi Noem continues to support Trump's policies moments after being dismissed from DHS
item.description: Kristi Noem, serving as the United States Secretary of Homeland Security, was abruptly dismissed by President Donald Trump during his second term. The firing was announced publicly while Noem was deli...

item.title: Gulf states face security and diplomatic challenges after US-Iran conflict escalates
item.description: The Gulf region is experiencing severe tensions following the US and Israel's bombing of Iran in retaliation for the killing of Iran’s supreme leader, Ali Khamenei. Iran responded with drone strikes t...

item.title: DILG directs attached agencies to implement fuel cost-cutting measures amid expected price surges
item.description: Due to anticipated further increases in fuel prices caused by the ongoing Middle East crisis, the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has instructed its attached agencies to adopt s...
